disease_associations.disease: Breast cancer (including hereditary breast and ovarian cancer syndrome, HBOC)
Ovarian cancer (high-grade serous / epithelial ovarian cancer)

cellular_states: Early embryonic cells during maternal-to-zygotic transition (MZT)
Germ layer formation and early tissue morphogenesis
ccRCC cells (BRCA1/2-WT, where BRCA1 is degraded via SLFN11)
pubmed.druggability_notes: BRCA1/2-mutated tumors are sensitive to PARP inhibitors (e.g., olaparib, niraparib, fluzoparib) via synthetic lethality; BRCA1/2-WT ccRCC is resistant to PARPi monotherapy but can be sensitized via SLFN11-dependent vulnerability with alkylating agents (TMZ, streptozocin). Novel BRCA1 variants in PCa cluster in DNA repair domains including the coiled-coil and RAD51-binding regions. SLFN11 drives proteasomal degradation of BRCA1 protein, inversely correlating with BRCA1 expression. PARP1-PARP2 trapping at replicative gaps is a determinant of PARPi sensitivity.

functions: E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase mediating 'Lys-6'-linked polyubiquitin chain formation
Central role in DNA repair and cellular responses to DNA damage
Component of BRCA1-BARD1 heterodimer coordinating DNA repair, ubiquitination and transcriptional regulation to maintain genomic stability
Regulates centrosomal microtubule nucleation
Required for S-phase and G2 cell cycle arrest after ionizing irradiation
Required for FANCD2 targeting to sites of DNA damage
Inhibits lipid synthesis by binding inactive phosphorylated ACACA and preventing its dephosphorylation
Contributes to homologous recombination repair via direct interaction with PALB2
pathways: Homologous DNA Pairing and Strand Exchange (Homologous Recombination Repair)
Cell Cycle Checkpoints (G2/M DNA damage checkpoint)
Transcriptional regulation by RNA polymerase II
cellular_states: Cells undergoing DNA damage response after ionizing radiation
S-phase and G2/M cell cycle arrest
uniprot.druggability_notes: No explicit druggability or binding pocket information stated; protein contains a zinc-finger (RING) domain conferring E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase (transferase) activity and binds zinc ions. Functions via BRCA1-BARD1 heterodimer interface and direct interactions with PALB2/BRCA2-RAD51 machinery, which are relevant to synthetic-lethal (e.g., HRR-deficiency) therapeutic contexts but not described as drug targets in this text.

opentargets.druggability_notes: No explicit druggability or binding-pocket information is provided in the text. BRCA1 loss-of-function induces HRR deficiency (defective HDR pathways noted), which is the mechanistic basis for synthetic-lethal PARP inhibitor sensitivity, though PARP inhibitors are not directly mentioned in this text.
